meta.category_disclaimer: The paper shelves are intentionally coarse: Theoretical and Mechanical Analysis, Architecture and Algorithm Designs, and Applications Focused. Foundation status plus Loop Mechanism / focus / domain tags carry secondary structure without introducing lineage buckets.
meta.blog_section_desc: Long-form technical posts, essays, and deep-dives about loop models. Blogs can carry Loop Mechanism / focus / domain tags but stay in a single flat section rather than the paper taxonomy.
meta.briefing_section_desc: Reader-facing daily research briefings: notable loop-model papers, watchlist candidates, and concise inclusion rationale.

analysis.title: Theoretical and Mechanical Analysis
analysis.desc: Analytical papers whose main artifact is understanding loop models: theory, mechanism analysis, diagnostics, formal properties, or interpretability.
analysis.readme_intro: Theoretical and Mechanical Analysis collects papers whose primary contribution is analysis: why loop models work, what formal properties they have, and what mechanisms they exhibit.

designs.title: Architecture and Algorithm Designs
designs.desc: Papers that propose loop-model architectures or algorithms, often to improve performance, efficiency, training, inference, or memory use.
designs.readme_intro: Architecture and Algorithm Designs collects the constructive side of the field: new looped architectures, algorithms, recurrent computation graphs, and efficiency or memory-compression methods.

applications.title: Applications Focused
applications.desc: Papers whose main reader takeaway is evidence that loop models work in a specific external domain or task, such as robotics, VLA, multimodal, tabular, or graph data.
applications.readme_intro: Applications Focused collects papers centered on applying loop models to concrete domains or tasks, including robotics, VLA, multimodal settings, tabular data, graph data, and other non-core benchmarks.

papers.title: Neural GPUs Learn Algorithms
papers.authors: Łukasz Kaiser, Ilya Sutskever
papers.desc: Introduces Neural GPUs, a recurrent convolutional architecture that learns parallel algorithms like addition and multiplication through repeated application of a shared convolutional recurrent block.

papers.title: Adaptive Computation Time for Recurrent Neural Networks
papers.authors: Alex Graves
papers.desc: Introduces ACT, allowing RNNs to learn how many computational steps to take per input, laying the groundwork for dynamic-depth recurrent computation.

papers.title: Universal Transformers
papers.authors: Mostafa Dehghani, Stephan Gouws, Oriol Vinyals, Jakob Uszkoreit, Łukasz Kaiser
papers.desc: Extends the standard Transformer with recurrent computation over depth via weight tying, enabling Turing-complete computation and combining the parallelism of Transformers with the inductive bias of RNNs.

papers.title: Deep Equilibrium Models
papers.authors: Shaojie Bai, J. Zico Kolter, Vladlen Koltun
papers.desc: Proposes to directly solve for the fixed point of an infinite-depth network, enabling implicit-depth models that are memory-efficient and theoretically equivalent to infinitely deep recurrent networks.

papers.title: Multiscale Deep Equilibrium Models
papers.authors: Shaojie Bai, Vladlen Koltun, J. Zico Kolter
papers.desc: Extends DEQ to multiscale hierarchical representations, achieving competitive performance on large-scale vision tasks.

papers.title: On Training Implicit Models
papers.authors: Zhengyang Geng, Xin-Yu Zhang, Shaojie Bai, Yisen Wang, Zhouchen Lin
papers.desc: Proposes phantom gradient, a lightweight backpropagation estimator for implicit (infinite-depth) models that uses damped unrolling and a truncated Neumann series to speed backward passes while matching or surpassing exact-gradient baselines on large-scale tasks.

papers.title: A Generalist Neural Algorithmic Learner
papers.authors: Borja Ibarz, Vitaly Kurin, George Papamakarios, Kyriacos Nikiforou, Mehdi Bennani, Róbert Csordás, Andrew Dudzik, Matko Bošnjak, Alex Vitvitskyi, Yulia Rubanova, Andreea Deac, Beatrice Bevilacqua, Yaroslav Ganin, Charles Blundell, Petar Veličković
papers.desc: Presents a single GNN model trained on 30+ algorithms from the CLRS benchmark, demonstrating that a shared recurrent architecture can generalize across diverse algorithmic tasks.

papers.title: Looped Transformers as Programmable Computers
papers.authors: Angeliki Giannou, Shashank Rajput, Jy-yong Sohn, Kangwook Lee, Jason D. Lee, Dimitris Papailiopoulos
papers.desc: Shows that a shallow looped transformer can emulate instruction-set computation and iterative algorithms such as SGD or matrix inversion, with the recurrence acting as a reusable program counter.

papers.title: CoTFormer: A Chain-of-Thought Driven Architecture with Budget-Adaptive Computation Cost at Inference
papers.authors: Amirkeivan Mohtashami, Matteo Pagliardini, Martin Jaggi
papers.desc: Recasts chain-of-thought as recurrent depth inside a token-level transformer, using token-wise adaptive computation to spend extra iterations only where additional reasoning budget helps.

papers.title: Looped Transformers are Better at Learning Learning Algorithms
papers.authors: Liu Yang, Kangwook Lee, Robert Nowak, Dimitris Papailiopoulos
papers.desc: Proposes looped-transformer training for in-context data-fitting tasks, showing comparable performance to standard transformers with under 10% of the parameters by better matching iterative learning algorithms.

papers.title: AlgoFormer: An Efficient Transformer Framework with Algorithmic Structures
papers.authors: Yihang Gao, Chuanyang Zheng, Enze Xie, Han Shi, Tianyang Hu, Yu Li, Michael K. Ng, Zhenguo Li, Zhaoqiang Liu
papers.desc: Splits computation into pre-, loop-, and post-transformer stages, showing that structured recurrent depth can outperform standard and vanilla looped transformers on algorithmic and language tasks.

papers.title: MoEUT: Mixture-of-Experts Universal Transformers
papers.authors: Róbert Csordás, Kazuki Irie, Jürgen Schmidhuber, Christopher Potts, Christopher D. Manning
papers.desc: Introduces MoEUT, a mixture-of-experts Universal Transformer that combines shared recurrent depth with expert routing to improve language modeling while using less compute and memory than comparable baselines.

papers.title: On Expressive Power of Looped Transformers: Theoretical Analysis and Enhancement via Timestep Encoding
papers.authors: Kevin Xu, Issei Sato
papers.desc: Analyzes the expressive power of looped transformers, derives approximation-rate limits, and shows that timestep encoding improves their function-approximation behavior.

papers.title: Relaxed Recursive Transformers: Effective Parameter Sharing with Layer-wise LoRA
papers.authors: Sangmin Bae, Adam Fisch, Hrayr Harutyunyan, Ziwei Ji, Seungyeon Kim, Tal Schuster
papers.desc: Presents Relaxed Recursive Transformers as a parameter-sharing conversion and uptraining recipe that turns pretrained LLMs into compact recursive models using layer tying and layer-wise LoRA while preserving performance and improving deployment efficiency.

papers.title: Scaling up Test-Time Compute with Latent Reasoning: A Recurrent Depth Approach
papers.authors: Jonas Geiping, Sean McLeish, Neel Jain, John Kirchenbauer, Siddharth Singh, Brian R. Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Abhinav Bhatele, Tom Goldstein
papers.desc: Presents Huginn, a recurrent-depth transformer (3.5B params) that iterates a single block up to 64 times per token, achieving strong reasoning performance that scales with additional test-time compute.

papers.title: Implicit Language Models are RNNs: Balancing Parallelization and Expressivity
papers.authors: Mark Schöne, Babak Rahmani, Heiner Kremer, Fabian Falck, Hitesh Ballani, Jannes Gladrow
papers.desc: Introduces implicit state-space language models that iterate a shared transition toward a fixed point, recovering RNN-like expressivity while retaining mostly parallel training.

papers.title: Reasoning with Latent Thoughts: On the Power of Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Nikunj Saunshi, Nishanth Dikkala, Zhiyuan Li, Sanjiv Kumar, Sashank J. Reddi
papers.desc: Studies looped transformers as reasoning models, showing effective-depth scaling, latent-thought simulation of chain-of-thought, and a looping-based regularizer that improves the reasoning-versus-memorization trade-off.

papers.title: Hierarchical Reasoning Model
papers.authors: Guan Wang, Jin Li, Yuhao Sun, Xing Chen, Changling Liu, Yue Wu, Meng Lu, Sen Song, Yasin Abbasi Yadkori
papers.desc: Proposes HRM, a brain-inspired recurrent architecture with two coupled modules at different timescales: a high-level module for abstract planning and a low-level module for detailed execution.

papers.title: Latent Chain-of-Thought? Decoding the Depth-Recurrent Transformer
papers.authors: Wenquan Lu, Yuechuan Yang, Kyle Lee, Yanshu Li, Enqi Liu
papers.desc: Probes a depth-recurrent Transformer to test whether latent chain-of-thought structure emerges across recurrence steps, finding limited evidence and recurrence-depth-dependent interpretability effects.

papers.title: Skip a Layer or Loop it? Test-Time Depth Adaptation of Pretrained LLMs
papers.authors: Ziyue Li, Yang Li, Tianyi Zhou
papers.desc: Proposes Chain-of-Layers (CoLa), an inference-time search method that skips or repeats pretrained LLM layers per sample via MCTS to improve efficiency and reasoning accuracy.

papers.title: Mixture-of-Recursions: Learning Dynamic Recursive Depths for Adaptive Token-Level Computation
papers.authors: Sangmin Bae, Yujin Kim, Reza Bayat, Sungnyun Kim, Jiyoun Ha, Tal Schuster, Adam Fisch, Hrayr Harutyunyan, Ziwei Ji, Aaron Courville, Se-Young Yun
papers.desc: Introduces Mixture-of-Recursions, a recursive transformer with token-level routing that adapts recursion depth and active-token attention so easy tokens exit early while hard tokens keep thinking.

papers.title: Two-Scale Latent Dynamics for Recurrent-Depth Transformers
papers.authors: Francesco Pappone, Donato Crisostomi, Emanuele Rodolà
papers.desc: Analyzes recurrent-depth transformers through a two-scale latent-dynamics lens, showing shrinking and increasingly orthogonal loop updates and deriving a second-order early-exit criterion that improves latency-quality trade-offs.

papers.title: Coevolutionary Continuous Discrete Diffusion: Make Your Diffusion Language Model a Latent Reasoner
papers.authors: Cai Zhou, Chenxiao Yang, Yi Hu, Chenyu Wang, Chubin Zhang, Muhan Zhang, Lester Mackey, Tommi Jaakkola, Stephen Bates, Dinghuai Zhang
papers.desc: Proposes Coevolutionary Continuous Discrete Diffusion, a joint continuous-discrete diffusion language model that repeatedly denoises latent and token states with one time-conditioned model, linking diffusion sampling to latent reasoning and looped-transformer expressivity.

papers.title: Less is More: Recursive Reasoning with Tiny Networks
papers.authors: Alexia Jolicoeur-Martineau
papers.desc: Proposes Tiny Recursive Model (TRM), a single tiny network that recursively refines latent state and answer over multiple improvement steps, outperforming HRM and many larger models on ARC-AGI-style reasoning tasks.

papers.title: Parallel Loop Transformer for Efficient Test-Time Computation Scaling
papers.authors: Bohong Wu, Mengzhao Chen, Xiang Luo, Shen Yan, Qifan Yu, Fan Xia, Tianqi Zhang, Hongrui Zhan, Zheng Zhong, Xun Zhou, Siyuan Qiao, Xingyan Bin
papers.desc: Introduces the Parallel Loop Transformer, which preserves looped-model accuracy while reducing latency and memory through cross-loop parallelism and shared-loop KV representations.

papers.title: Scaling Latent Reasoning via Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Rui-Jie Zhu, Zixuan Wang, Kai Hua, Tianyu Zhang, Ziniu Li, Haoran Que, Boyi Wei, Zixin Wen, Fan Yin, He Xing, Lu Li, Jiajun Shi, Kaijing Ma, Shanda Li, Taylor Kergan, Andrew Smith, Xingwei Qu, Mude Hui, Bohong Wu, Qiyang Min, Hongzhi Huang, Xun Zhou, Wei Ye, Jiaheng Liu, Jian Yang, Yunfeng Shi, Chenghua Lin, Enduo Zhao, Tianle Cai, Ge Zhang, Wenhao Huang, Yoshua Bengio, Jason Eshraghian
papers.desc: Introduces Ouro, a family of pre-trained Looped Language Models (1.4B and 2.6B) that match the performance of 12B standard LLMs. Establishes loop depth as a third scaling axis beyond model size and data.

papers.title: Teaching Pretrained Language Models to Think Deeper with Retrofitted Recurrence
papers.authors: Sean McLeish, Ang Li, John Kirchenbauer, Dayal Singh Kalra, Brian R. Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Avi Schwarzschild, Jonas Geiping, Tom Goldstein, Micah Goldblum
papers.desc: A framework for retrofitting pretrained feedforward language models with depth recurrence, improving training efficiency for depth-recurrent models and enabling greater FLOP efficiency than comparable feedforward models.

papers.title: Think-at-Hard: Selective Latent Iterations to Improve Reasoning Language Models
papers.authors: Tianyu Fu, Yichen You, Zekai Chen, Guohao Dai, Huazhong Yang, Yu Wang
papers.desc: Introduces Think-at-Hard, a dynamic latent-thinking method that uses a learned decider to apply extra recurrent latent iterations only to hard tokens, with LoRA refiners and duo-causal attention across iteration depth.

papers.title: Universal Reasoning Model
papers.authors: Zitian Gao, Lynx Chen, Yihao Xiao, He Xing, Ran Tao, Haoming Luo, Joey Zhou, Bryan Dai
papers.desc: Proposes URM, a Universal Transformer-based architecture with weight tying that beats standard transformers on reasoning benchmarks through iterative depth computation.

papers.title: Depth-Recurrent Attention Mixtures: Giving Latent Reasoning the Attention it Deserves
papers.authors: Jonas Knupp, Jan Hendrik Metzen, Jeremias Bohn, Georg Groh, Kristian Kersting
papers.desc: Introduces a modular framework combining sequence attention and depth attention for recurrent-depth models, improving FLOP-, parameter-, and memory-efficiency simultaneously.

papers.title: Inverse Depth Scaling From Most Layers Being Similar
papers.authors: Yizhou Liu, Sara Kangaslahti, Ziming Liu, Jeff Gore
papers.desc: Analyzes LLMs and toy residual networks to show loss scales inversely with depth when many layers are functionally similar and primarily reduce error via ensemble averaging.

papers.title: On the Role of Iterative Computation in Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Raj Ghugare, Michał Bortkiewicz, Alicja Ziarko, Benjamin Eysenbach
papers.desc: Formalizes compute-bounded RL policies and introduces a minimal variable-compute architecture, showing that extra iterative computation improves performance and longer-horizon generalization across 31 online and offline RL tasks.

papers.title: Recurrent-Depth VLA: Implicit Test-Time Compute Scaling of Vision-Language-Action Models via Latent Iterative Reasoning
papers.authors: Yalcin Tur, Jalal Naghiyev, Haoquan Fang, Wei-Chuan Tsai, Jiafei Duan, Dieter Fox, Ranjay Krishna
papers.desc: Introduces RD-VLA, a vision-language-action architecture with a weight-tied recurrent action head and adaptive stopping, enabling latent test-time compute scaling for robotics with constant memory footprint.

papers.title: Understanding Dynamic Compute Allocation in Recurrent Transformers
papers.authors: Ibraheem Muhammad Moosa, Suhas Lohit, Ye Wang, Moitreya Chatterjee, Wenpeng Yin
papers.desc: Proposes ANIRA, a recurrent Transformer framework for per-token variable-depth computation, and shows adaptive compute can align with token complexity while failing to extrapolate to longer algorithmic inputs.

papers.title: Looping Back to Move Forward: Recursive Transformers for Efficient and Flexible Large Multimodal Models
papers.authors: Ruihan Xu, Yuting Gao, Lan Wang, Jianing Li, Weihao Chen, Qingpei Guo, Ming Yang, Shiliang Zhang
papers.desc: Introduces RecursiveVLM, a recursive multimodal transformer with a recursive connector and monotonic recursion loss that enables on-demand extra refinement under varying compute budgets.

papers.title: Prioritize the Process, Not Just the Outcome: Rewarding Latent Thought Trajectories Improves Reasoning in Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Jonathan Williams, Esin Tureci
papers.desc: Introduces RLTT, a reinforcement-learning objective that assigns reward across the full latent thought trajectory of looped language models rather than only the final latent state.

papers.title: LoopFormer: Elastic-Depth Looped Transformers for Latent Reasoning via Shortcut Modulation
papers.authors: Ahmadreza Jeddi, Marco Ciccone, Babak Taati
papers.desc: Introduces LoopFormer, trained on variable-length trajectories to enable budget-conditioned reasoning. Uses shortcut-consistency regularization to ensure stable internal trajectories across different loop depths.

papers.title: SpiralFormer: Looped Transformers Can Learn Hierarchical Dependencies via Multi-Resolution Recursion
papers.authors: Chengting Yu, Xiaobo Shu, Yadao Wang, Yizhen Zhang, Haoyi Wu, You Wu, Rujiao Long, Ziheng Chen, Yuchi Xu, Wenbo Su, Bo Zheng
papers.desc: Introduces SpiralFormer, a looped transformer that applies shared layers under a multi-resolution recursion schedule to learn hierarchical dependencies more efficiently than fixed-resolution recurrent baselines.

papers.title: AdaPonderLM: Gated Pondering Language Models with Token-Wise Adaptive Depth
papers.authors: Shixiang Song, He Li, Zitong Wang, Boyi Zeng, Feichen Song, Yixuan Wang, Zhiqin John Xu, Ziwei He, Zhouhan Lin
papers.desc: Introduces AdaPonderLM, a self-supervised recurrent language model with token-wise halting gates and KV reuse, allocating more loop steps to hard tokens under a fixed compute budget.

papers.title: Mixture of Universal Experts: Scaling Virtual Width via Depth-Width Transformation
papers.authors: Yilong Chen, Naibin Gu, Junyuan Shang, Zhenyu Zhang, Yuchen Feng, Jiawei Sheng, Tingwen Liu, Shuohuan Wang, Yu Sun, Hua Wu, Haifeng Wang
papers.desc: Proposes MOUE, which reuses a universal layer-agnostic expert pool across layers to transform depth into virtual width and improve MoE performance under fixed activation budgets.

papers.title: Recursive Inference Machines for Neural Reasoning
papers.authors: Mieszko Komisarczyk, Saurabh Mathur, Maurice Kraus, Sriraam Natarajan, Kristian Kersting
papers.desc: Introduces Recursive Inference Machines, a recurrent reasoning framework that casts TRMs as a special case and improves ARC-AGI, Sudoku, and tabular classification by reweighting the history of loop states.

papers.title: Tiny Autoregressive Recursive Models
papers.authors: Paulius Rauba, Claudio Fanconi, Mihaela van der Schaar
papers.desc: Studies autoregressive Tiny Recursive Models under compute-matched baselines, finding that simple two-step refinement helps on small algorithmic tasks while the full Autoregressive TRM shows no reliable gains.

papers.title: Adaptive Loops and Memory in Transformers: Think Harder or Know More?
papers.authors: Markus Frey, Behzad Shomali, Ali Hamza Bashir, David Berghaus, Joachim Koehler, Mehdi Ali
papers.desc: Introduces transformers with adaptive per-layer looping and gated memory banks, showing that combining learned halting with extra storage improves reasoning under matched parameter and FLOP budgets.

papers.title: LoopRPT: Reinforcement Pre-Training for Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Guo Tang, Shixin Jiang, Heng Chang, Nuo Chen, Yuhan Li, Huiming Fan, Jia Li, Ming Liu, Bing Qin
papers.desc: Proposes LoopRPT, a reinforcement pre-training method for looped language models that assigns learning signals to latent iterations, improving accuracy-compute trade-offs and strengthening early-stage reasoning on Ouro.

papers.title: Thinking Deeper, Not Longer: Depth-Recurrent Transformers for Compositional Generalization
papers.authors: Hung-Hsuan Chen
papers.desc: Introduces a depth-recurrent Transformer for compositional generalization, with silent thinking, LayerScale, and identity-biased recurrence enabling stable deep latent iteration.

papers.title: Loop, Think, & Generalize: Implicit Reasoning in Recurrent-Depth Transformers
papers.authors: Harsh Kohli, Srinivasan Parthasarathy, Huan Sun, Yuekun Yao
papers.desc: Studies implicit reasoning in recurrent-depth transformers, showing that iterating shared transformer layers can unlock systematic generalization and depth extrapolation while also exposing overthinking limits.

papers.title: ELT: Elastic Looped Transformers for Visual Generation
papers.authors: Sahil Goyal, Swayam Agrawal, Gautham Govind Anil, Prateek Jain, Sujoy Paul, Aditya Kusupati
papers.desc: Introduces Elastic Looped Transformers for image and video generation, using weight-shared recurrent transformer blocks plus Intra-Loop Self Distillation to support any-time inference with dynamic quality-compute trade-offs from a single training run.

papers.title: Relational Preference Encoding in Looped Transformer Internal States
papers.authors: Jan Kirin
papers.desc: Probes looped transformer hidden states during iterative refinement, showing that human-preference information is encoded primarily in relational differences between loop states rather than independent per-state scores.

papers.title: A Deep Equilibrium Network for Hyperspectral Unmixing
papers.authors: Chentong Wang, Jincheng Gao, Fei Zhu, Jie Chen
papers.desc: Recasts hyperspectral unmixing as a deep equilibrium model, replacing the reconstruction-gradient operator with a trainable convolutional update and solving for an implicit fixed point with constant-memory differentiation.

papers.title: A Mechanistic Analysis of Looped Reasoning Language Models
papers.authors: Hugh Blayney, Álvaro Arroyo, Johan Obando-Ceron, Pablo Samuel Castro, Aaron Courville, Michael M. Bronstein, Xiaowen Dong
papers.desc: Analyzes looped reasoning LLMs mechanistically, showing recurrent cycles converge to layer-specific fixed points and that feedforward-like inference stages repeat across latent recurrences.

papers.title: Parcae: Scaling Laws For Stable Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Hayden Prairie, Zachary Novack, Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick, Daniel Y. Fu
papers.desc: Introduces Parcae, a stable looped language model that constrains injection spectral norms to prevent instability and studies isoFLOPs-style training- and test-time scaling laws for quality gains under fixed-parameter budgets.

papers.title: Hierarchical vs. Flat Iteration in Shared-Weight Transformers
papers.authors: Sang-Il Han
papers.desc: Empirically compares hierarchical shared-weight recurrence against flat shared-weight iteration and independent-layer stacking, revealing a persistent representational gap for the recurrent hierarchy.

papers.title: Stability and Generalization in Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Asher Labovich
papers.desc: Analyzes stability and generalization in looped transformers through a fixed-point framework, characterizing when recall and normalization yield reachable, input-dependent, and trainable loop dynamics.

papers.title: LASER: Low-Rank Activation SVD for Efficient Recursion
papers.authors: Ege Çakar, Ketan Ali Raghu, Lia Zheng
papers.desc: Analyzes Tiny Recursive Model activation geometry during recursive unrolling and introduces LASER, a dynamic low-rank activation compression method that cuts recursive activation memory by ~60% without statistically significant accuracy loss.

papers.title: One Step Forward and K Steps Back: Better Reasoning with Denoising Recursion Models
papers.authors: Chris Cameron, Wangzheng Wang, Nikita Ivanov, Ashmita Bhattacharyya, Didier Chételat, Yingxue Zhang
papers.desc: Introduces Denoising Recursion Models, a looped-transformer training method that corrupts targets and trains recursive refinement over multiple steps, improving ARC-AGI reasoning over TRM.

papers.title: How Much Is One Recurrence Worth? Iso-Depth Scaling Laws for Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Kristian Schwethelm, Daniel Rueckert, Georgios Kaissis
papers.desc: Measures the parameter value of recurrence in looped language models with iso-depth scaling laws, estimating how extra recurrent passes trade off against unique depth and training compute.

papers.title: Hyperloop Transformers
papers.authors: Abbas Zeitoun, Lucas Torroba-Hennigen, Yoon Kim
papers.desc: Introduces Hyperloop Transformers, a parameter-efficient looped Transformer that applies only a middle block recurrently and adds hyper-connections between loops to improve memory-efficient language modeling.

papers.title: Universal Transformers Need Memory: Depth-State Trade-offs in Adaptive Recursive Reasoning
papers.authors: Grigory Sapunov
papers.desc: Studies a single-block Universal Transformer with ACT on Sudoku-Extreme, showing that learned memory tokens are necessary for non-trivial recursive-depth reasoning and that ACT initialization can trap the model in shallow computation.

papers.title: On Halting vs Converging in Recurrent Graph Neural Networks
papers.authors: Jeroen Bollen, Stijn Vansummeren
papers.desc: Analyzes recurrent graph neural networks that repeatedly apply message passing until convergence or halting, proving expressiveness relationships between converging, output-converging, and halting RGNN variants.

papers.title: ITS-Mina: A Harris Hawks Optimization-Based All-MLP Framework with Iterative Refinement and External Attention for Multivariate Time Series Forecasting
papers.authors: Pourya Zamanvaziri, Amirhossein Sadr, Aida Pakniyat, Dara Rahmati
papers.desc: Applies a shared-parameter iterative refinement module inside an all-MLP multivariate time-series forecasting system, using the loop-model pattern for a concrete forecasting application.

papers.title: Is One Layer Enough? Understanding Inference Dynamics in Tabular Foundation Models
papers.authors: Amir Rezaei Balef, Mykhailo Koshil, Katharina Eggensperger
papers.desc: Analyzes layerwise inference dynamics in tabular foundation models and uses the observed depth redundancy to build a looped single-layer model that preserves comparable performance with about 20% of the original parameters.

papers.title: Transformers Efficiently Perform In-Context Logistic Regression via Normalized Gradient Descent
papers.authors: Chenyang Zhang, Yuan Cao
papers.desc: Proves that softmax transformers can implement in-context logistic regression by treating layers as normalized-gradient-descent steps, then trains one self-attention layer and applies it recurrently as a looped model with convergence and OOD guarantees.

papers.title: Bifurcation Models: Learning Set-Valued Solution Maps with Weight-Tied Dynamics
papers.authors: Caleb Jore, Jialin Liu
papers.desc: Studies weight-tied dynamics for set-valued solution maps, proving that regular equilibrium dynamics can represent multiple branches while repeated shared-operator iterations discover multiple valid equilibria on Ising and Allen-Cahn tasks.

papers.title: Memory-Efficient Looped Transformer: Decoupling Compute from Memory in Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Victor Conchello Vendrell, Arnau Padres Masdemont, Niccolò Grillo, Jordi Ros-Giralt, Arash Behboodi, Fabio Valerio Massoli
papers.desc: Memory-Efficient Looped Transformer enables constant‑memory iterative reasoning by sharing a single KV cache across loops, achieving strong performance without the linear memory scaling of prior looped LLMs.

papers.title: Sparse Layers are Critical to Scaling Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Ryan Lee, Jacob Biloki, Edward J. Hu, Jonathan May
papers.desc: Shows that MoE-style sparse layers can make looped language models scale better than dense looped transformers, with routing divergence across repeated shared layers recovering expressivity and loop boundaries serving as effective early-exit points.

papers.title: Quantum Injection Pathways for Implicit Graph Neural Networks
papers.authors: Pengyuan Xu, Tristan Zaborniak, Luis F. Rivera, Hausi A. Müller
papers.desc: Formulates quantum-signal injection pathways for graph deep-equilibrium models, comparing fixed, state-dependent, and backbone-dependent coupling inside the fixed-point operator with contraction guarantees and graph-classification experiments.

papers.title: LoopUS: Recasting Pretrained LLMs into Looped Latent Refinement Models
papers.authors: Taekhyun Park, Yongjae Lee, Dohee Kim, Hyerim Bae
papers.desc: Converts pretrained LLMs into encoder, looped reasoning block, and decoder components, using selective gating, random deep supervision, and adaptive early exiting to stabilize latent looping without training recurrent models from scratch.

papers.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
papers.authors: Jacob Fein-Ashley, Paria Rashidinejad
papers.desc: Introduces Attractor Models, where a backbone proposes output embeddings and an attractor module iteratively solves a fixed point with implicit differentiation, improving looped language modeling and small-model reasoning while allowing adaptive convergence-depth inference.

papers.title: Recurrent Transformer-Based Near- and Far-Field THz Wideband Channel Estimation for UM-MIMO
papers.authors: Dmitry Artemasov, Alexander Shmatok, Kirill Andreev, Alexey Frolov, Manjesh K. Hanawal, Nikola Zlatanov
papers.desc: Applies a block-recurrent transformer to hybrid near/far-field THz UM-MIMO channel estimation, training one state-memory transformer block once and iteratively reusing it to improve narrowband and wideband NMSE.

papers.title: Looped SSMs: Depth-Recurrence and Input Reshaping for Time Series Classification
papers.authors: Mónika Farsang, Ramin Hasani, Daniela Rus, Radu Grosu
papers.desc: Extends looped-transformer depth recurrence to state-space models by reusing the same SSM block across depth and adding input reshaping, showing tied-depth SSMs match or beat untied SSMs on six time-series benchmarks despite fewer parameters.

papers.title: One Model, Two Roles: Emergent Specialization in a Shared Recurrent Transformer
papers.authors: Jucheng Shen, Barbara Su, Anastasios Kyrillidis
papers.desc: Analyzes Asymmetric Input Recurrence, a two-state shared-weight recurrent Transformer where the same model updates L/H states, showing that state identity and input-injection asymmetry induce distinct proposal-vs-uncertainty roles on Sudoku-Extreme and Maze.

papers.title: PERL: Parameter Efficient Reasoning in CLIP Latent Space
papers.authors: Simone Carnemolla, Salvatore Calcagno, Daniela Giordano, Concetto Spampinato, Matteo Pennisi
papers.desc: Introduces PERL, a few-shot CLIP adaptation framework that reuses a compact shared reasoning module across latent refinement steps, improving base-to-novel, transfer, and OOD results with about 6K trainable parameters.

papers.title: Simply Stabilizing the Loop via Fully Looped Transformer
papers.authors: Rao Fu, Zixuan Yang, Jiankun Zhang, Jing Ma, Hechang Chen, Yu Li, Yi Chang
papers.desc: Stabilizes looped transformers with parameter-free fully looped signal routing and attention injection, enabling stable training at higher loop counts while preserving test-time loop-depth control.

papers.title: Generative Recursive Reasoning
papers.authors: Junyeob Baek, Mingyu Jo, Minsu Kim, Mengye Ren, Yoshua Bengio, Sungjin Ahn
papers.desc: Introduces GRAM, a probabilistic recursive-reasoning framework that models reasoning as stochastic latent trajectories, enabling multi-hypothesis computation, variational training, and inference-time scaling through depth and parallel sampling.

papers.title: i-DEQ: A stable inertial deep equilibrium model for image restoration
papers.authors: Antonin Clerc, Marien Renaud, Baudouin Denis De Seneville, Nicolas Papadakis
papers.desc: Introduces i-DEQ, an inertial deep-equilibrium image-restoration model that learns explicit nonconvex regularization and uses momentum in fixed-point iterations, improving stability and robustness while roughly halving DEQ inference time.

papers.title: Probabilistic Tiny Recursive Model
papers.authors: Amin Sghaier, Ali Parviz, Alexia Jolicoeur-Martineau
papers.desc: Introduces PTRM, an inference-time scaling framework for Tiny Recursive Models that injects Gaussian noise into recursive latent updates, runs parallel trajectories, and selects the final answer with the model's Q head without retraining.

papers.title: Nonlocal operator learning for fMRI encoding and decoding tasks
papers.authors: Andreas Kramer, Saugat Acharya, Alice Giola, Emanuele Zappala
papers.desc: Applies a latent neural integral-operator model to fMRI encoding and decoding, using fixed-point iterations in an auxiliary latent space before downstream classification or stimulus prediction.

papers.title: LT2: Linear-Time Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Chunyuan Deng, Yizhe Zhang, Rui-Jie Zhu, Yuanyuan Xu, Jiarui Liu, T. S. Eugene Ng, Hanjie Chen
papers.desc: Introduces LT2, a looped-transformer family that replaces quadratic attention with linear or sparse attention so repeated loop steps refine memory and expand receptive field while keeping inference more scalable.

papers.title: Interaction Locality in Hierarchical Recursive Reasoning
papers.authors: Yosuke Miyanishi, Tetsuro Morimura
papers.desc: Proposes interaction locality as a mechanistic measurement framework for HRM and TRM, showing how repeated recursive updates accumulate local writes into broader solution structure on grid reasoning benchmarks.

papers.title: Equilibrium Reasoners: Learning Attractors Enables Scalable Reasoning
papers.authors: Benhao Huang, Zhengyang Geng, Zico Kolter
papers.desc: Formalizes Equilibrium Reasoners as learned latent dynamical systems whose repeated update rule converges toward task-conditioned attractors, enabling depth and breadth test-time scaling for reasoning.

papers.title: Training-Free Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Lizhang Chen, Jonathan Li, Chen Liang, Ni Lao, Qiang Liu
papers.desc: Retrofits frozen pretrained transformers with a training-free inference wrapper that repeatedly applies a contiguous mid-stack layer block as damped refinement sub-steps, improving several QA and reasoning benchmarks without fine-tuning.

papers.title: Looped Diffusion Language Models
papers.authors: Sanghyun Lee, Chunsan Hong, Seungryong Kim, Jonghyun Lee, Jongho Park, Dongmin Park
papers.desc: Introduces LoopMDM, selectively looping early-middle transformer layers in masked diffusion language models so training gains depth-scaling without extra parameters and inference can vary loop count for compute scaling.

papers.title: Stabilizing Recurrent Dynamics for Test-Time Scalable Latent Reasoning in Looped Language Models
papers.authors: Xiao-Wen Yang, Ziyu Han, Xi-Hua Zhang, Wen-Da Wei, Jie-Jing Shao, Lan-Zhe Guo, Yu-Feng Li
papers.desc: Analyzes why Looped Language Models can collapse at larger recurrence depths and proposes STARS, a spectral-radius-regularized training framework that pushes latent dynamics toward stable fixed points for reliable test-time scaling.

papers.title: CosmicFish-HRM: Adaptive Reasoning via Hierarchical Recurrent Mechanisms in Compact Language Models
papers.authors: Venkat Akhil Lakkapragada
papers.desc: Explores a compact autoregressive language model with a Hierarchical Reasoning Module that iterates through high-level and low-level reasoning cycles and learns input-dependent halting behavior for adaptive reasoning depth.

papers.title: Déjà View: Looping Transformers for Multi-View 3D Reconstruction
papers.authors: Alessandro Burzio, Tobias Fischer, Sven Elflein, Qunjie Zhou, Riccardo de Lutio, Jiawei Ren, Jiahui Huang, Shengyu Huang, Marc Pollefeys, Laura Leal-Taixé, Zan Gojcic, Haithem Turki
papers.desc: Applies a single looped transformer block recurrently to per-view features for a variable number of refinement steps in multi-view 3D reconstruction, exposing loop count as an inference-time compute knob.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought and Compressed Looped Transformers: A Memory-Budget Separation
papers.authors: Haozhou Zhang
papers.desc: Compares chain-of-thought scratchpads with compressed looped Transformers, arguing that looped hidden-state recurrence is bounded by its persistent memory budget even when more recurrent computation is applied.

papers.title: Fixed-Point Masked Generative Modeling
papers.authors: Andrea Miele, Yiming Qin, Alba Carballo-Castro, Justin Deschenaux, Pascal Frossard
papers.desc: Replaces part of a masked generative model denoiser with a fixed-point solver over shared attention layers, using consistency training and solver-state reuse to adapt depth with fewer parameters.

papers.authors: Zhilong Zhang, Xinhui Zhang, Gongyu Jin, Sihua Wang, Danpu Liu, Changchuan Yin
papers.desc: Uses a recursive ViT structure to iteratively refine semantic features for image semantic communication while dynamically adjusting recursive depth and width under image and channel conditions.

papers.title: Looped Transformers with Layer Normalization Provably Learn the Power Method
papers.authors: Lyumin Wu, Chenyang Zhang, Yuan Cao
papers.desc: Proves that a looped linear transformer with layer normalization, trained only for principal component prediction, converges to a solution implementing the power method, with each self-attention layer performing one power iteration.

papers.title: CART: Context-Anchored Recurrent Transformer -- A Parameter-Efficient Architecture with Learned Stability
papers.authors: Chad A. Capps
papers.desc: Introduces a compact language model that reuses a single shared transformer core across depth while anchoring recurrence to precomputed key-value tensors and reports a mostly negative parameter-parity result against dense baselines.

papers.title: BA-T: An Iterative Transformer for Two-View Bundle Adjustment
papers.authors: Ganlin Zhang, Weirong Chen, Daniel Cremers, Xi Wang
papers.desc: Implements bundle-adjustment-style 3D reconstruction updates as a repeatable lightweight Transformer layer in implicit token space, improving pose and reconstruction estimates across iterations.

papers.title: When to Re-Plan: Subgoal Persistence in Hierarchical Latent Reasoning
papers.authors: Ayushi Chadha
papers.desc: Extends hierarchical latent reasoning with a slow manager module whose directional subgoal persists across several low-level hidden-state update steps, making subgoal duration a central design knob.

papers.title: LoopMoE: Unifying Iterative Computation with Mixture-of-Experts for Language Modeling
papers.authors: Wenkai Chen, Tianshu Li, Wenyong Huang, Yichun Yin, Lifeng Shang, Chengwei Qin
papers.desc: Introduces LoopMoE, a looped mixture-of-experts language model that combines sparse routing with iterative weight-shared computation through iteration-conditioned modulation and capacity balancing.

papers.title: Test-Time Compute Scaling for ASR with Depth-Conditioned Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Yacouba Kaloga, Shashi Kumar, Shakeel A. Sheikh, Driss Khalil, Petr Motlicek, Ina Kodrasi
papers.desc: Introduces LARM, a depth-conditioned looped Transformer for automatic speech recognition that reuses a shared acoustic-encoder block recurrently and scales recognition quality by increasing inference-time loop count.

papers.authors: Boyang Zhang, Lianlei Shan
papers.desc: Introduces a VLA latent-reasoning policy that initializes multiple latent hypotheses, refines them for K weight-tied steps, and aggregates them before action decoding.

papers.authors: Ziyue Li, Yang Li, Tianyi Zhou
papers.desc: Learns input-specific programs over pretrained LLM layers, allowing layers to be skipped or repeated so inference can use shorter or looped execution paths than the fixed-depth baseline.

papers.authors: Yunhong Lou, Xihang Yue, Xinran Wei, Tianqi Deng, Linchao Zhu
papers.desc: Introduces HamEvo, a fixed-point neural operator for Kohn-Sham Hamiltonian prediction that learns a single-step self-consistent update and returns the converged Hamiltonian as the learned operator's fixed point, improving electronic-structure prediction accuracy and transfer.

papers.title: LoopCoder-v2: Only Loop Once for Efficient Test-Time Computation Scaling
papers.authors: Jian Yang, Shawn Guo, Wei Zhang, Tianyu Zheng, Yaxin Du, Haau-Sing Li, Jiajun Wu, Yue Song, Yan Xing, Qingsong Cai, Zelong Huang, Chuan Hao, Ran Tao, Xianglong Liu, Wayne Xin Zhao, Mingjie Tang, Weifeng Lv, Ming Zhou, Bryan Dai
papers.desc: Studies loop-count selection in Parallel Loop Transformers by training LoopCoder-v2 coder models with shared looped blocks, finding that two loops give the main productive refinement while additional loops regress under cross-loop positional mismatch.

papers.title: Fixed-Point Reasoners: Stable and Adaptive Deep Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Sajad Movahedi, Vera Milovanović, Shlomo Libo Feigin, Alexander Theus, Thomas Hofmann, Valentina Boeva, T. Konstantin Rusch, Antonio Orvieto
papers.desc: Introduces FPRM, a Transformer-based Fixed-Point Reasoning Model that repeatedly applies a looped reasoning block and uses fixed-point convergence as an end-to-end halting signal, adapting test-time compute to task difficulty on Sudoku, Maze, state-tracking, and ARC-AGI benchmarks.

papers.title: Looped World Models
papers.authors: Hongyuan Adam Lu, Z. L. Victor Wei, Qun Zhang, Jinrui Zeng, Bowen Cao, Lingwei Meng, Mocheng Li, Zezhong Wang, Haonan Yin, Naifu Xue, Minyu Chen, Cenyuan Zhang, Zefan Zhang, Hao Wei, Jiawei Zhou, Haoran Xu, Hao Yang, Ronglai Zuo, Tongda Xu, Yonghao Li, Jian Chen, Hebin Wang, Zeyu Gao, Yang Li, Wei Zhao, Qimin Zhong, Siqi Liu, Yumeng Zhang, Leyan Cui, Zhangyu Wang, Wai Lam
papers.desc: Introduces LoopWM, a world-model architecture that iteratively refines latent environment states through a parameter-shared Transformer block, using adaptive computation to scale latent depth per prediction step and reporting up to 100x parameter efficiency over conventional world-modeling approaches.

papers.title: On the Residual Scaling of Looped Transformers: Stability and Transferability
papers.authors: Shaowen Wang, Bingrui Li, Ge Zhang, Wenhao Huang, Shen Yan, Jian Li
papers.desc: Analyzes residual scaling in looped weight-tied Transformers, showing that correlated shared-block updates require stronger 1/N residual scaling across loop count and enable hyperparameter transfer from small to large recurrent depths.

papers.title: Recurrent neural networks approximate continuous functions
papers.authors: Valentin Abadie, Clemens Hutter, Helmut Bölcskei
papers.desc: Proves that a single fixed-weight ReLU recurrent neural network with fixed hidden dimension can approximate any continuous function by running its shared recurrent dynamics longer, with runtime becoming the resource that improves accuracy.

papers.title: Adaptive Recurrent Message Passing for Test Time Computing on Graphs
papers.authors: Junshu Sun, Wanxing Chang, Qingming Huang, Shuhui Wang
papers.desc: Introduces AdaR, an adaptive recurrent graph model that repeatedly applies learned recurrent message-passing updates with step conditioning, enabling flexible test-time compute on graph tasks without changing model parameters.

papers.title: What Survives When You Compress a Recursive Reasoner for the Edge?
papers.authors: Pearse Jim, Steven Kolawole, Opegbemi Matthias Busoye, Glory Bagai, Virginia Smith
papers.desc: Analyzes compression of recursive reasoning models that repeatedly update latent state, showing how quantization and pruning can preserve local predictions while destroying global reasoning trajectories and how calibrated INT4 recovers them for edge deployment.

papers.title: Stabilizing Extrapolation in Looped Transformers via Learned Stochastic Stopping
papers.authors: Hsun-Yu Kuo, El Mahdi Chayti, Patrik Reizinger, Wieland Brendel, Martin Jaggi
papers.desc: Studies looped Transformers that repeatedly apply a shared Transformer block, showing that stochastic training-time loop counts and learned stochastic stopping can reduce out-of-distribution variance across inference loop counts on algorithmic sequence tasks.

papers.title: Bridging the Gap Between Latent and Explicit Reasoning with Looped Transformers
papers.authors: Ying Fan, Anej Svete, Kangwook Lee
papers.desc: Introduces LOTUS, a looped padded Transformer that reuses recurrent-depth weights for parallel latent chain-of-thought blocks, matching explicit CoT at 3B scale while reducing thought-phase latency.

papers.title: CHERRY: Compressed Hierarchical Experts with Recurrent Representational Yield
papers.authors: Dohyeon Kwon, Youngjin Park
papers.desc: Compresses a 48-layer language Transformer to 6 layers and restores depth through learned recurrent unrolling, then combines compressed recurrent models as efficient experts.

papers.title: DiscoLoop: Looping Discrete Embeddings and Continuous Hidden States for Multi-hop Reasoning
papers.authors: Hengyu Fu, Tianyu Guo, Zixuan Wang, Hanlin Zhu, Jason D. Lee, Jiantao Jiao, Stuart Russell, Song Mei
papers.desc: Introduces DiscoLoop, a looping architecture for multi-hop reasoning that carries both discrete embedding and continuous hidden-state channels across recurrent loop steps, improving generalization and training efficiency over looped-transformer baselines.

papers.title: G-RRM: Guiding Symbolic Solvers with Recurrent Reasoning Models
papers.authors: Timo Bertram, Sidhant Bhavnani, Richard Freinschlag, Erich Kobler, Andreas Mayr, Günter Klambauer
papers.desc: Integrates symbol-equivariant Recurrent Reasoning Models with symbolic solvers, showing that neural solution proposals can cut Sudoku and SAT-style search when the downstream solver can overwrite imperfect recurrent-model hints.

papers.title: HRM-Text: Efficient Pretraining Beyond Scaling
papers.authors: Guan Wang, Changling Liu, Chenyu Wang, Cai Zhou, Yuhao Sun, Yifei Wu, Shuai Zhen, Luca Scimeca, Yasin Abbasi Yadkori
papers.desc: Introduces HRM-Text, a 1B Hierarchical Recurrent Model language model that combines dual-timescale recurrent Transformer modules with MagicNorm, warmup deep credit assignment, PrefixLM masking, and task-completion pretraining for efficient training from 40B unique tokens.

papers.title: ChainGPT: Dual-Reasoning Model with Recurrent Depth and Multi-Rank State Updates
papers.authors: Yunao Zheng, Xiaojie Wang, Lei Ren, Chen Wei
papers.desc: Introduces ChainGPT, a dual-reasoning recurrent-depth architecture that combines multi-substep state updates and state-guided sparse attention to move reasoning into latent computation, with adaptive stopping as a supporting mechanism.

papers.title: MoDr: Mixture-of-Depth-Recurrent Transformers for Test-Time Reasoning
papers.authors: Xiaojing Zhang, Haifeng Wu, Gang He, Jiyang Shen, Bochen Lyu, Zhanxing Zhu
papers.desc: Introduces MoDr, which adds multi-branch routing to a depth-recurrent Transformer so looped models can explore solution paths more adaptively at test time.

blogs.title: Adaptive Computation Time (ACT) in Neural Networks [3/3]
blogs.desc: Reviews Adaptive Computation Time in transformer-style models, focusing on Universal Transformers with dynamic per-position halting, adaptive attention span, and ALBERT-style cross-layer parameter sharing as related forms of adaptive or repeated computation.

blogs.title: Looped-GPT: Looping During Pre-training improves Generalization
blogs.desc: Introduces Looped-GPT, a reverse-residual depth-recurrent GPT variant, and reports pre-training experiments where looped models improve generalization under matched parameter, token, and fixed-FLOPs settings.

blogs.title: Loop-Model FLOPs and Memory in an Ablation Chain
blogs.desc: Builds a clean cost-ablation chain for loop-model training, comparing shared versus non-shared weights, per-step losses, detach, instant updates, internal truncation, and gradient checkpointing, then checks the resulting FLOPs and memory trade-offs with a toy benchmark.

blogs.title: On the Looped Transformers Controversy
blogs.desc: Argues that benchmark patterns and serving-compute constraints make deterministic weight-tied looping a plausible explanation for Claude Mythos-like gains, while explicitly framing the claim as speculation rather than confirmation.

blogs.title: Claude Mythos, Looped LLM, and the Depth Scaling Axis
blogs.desc: Analyzes why Claude Mythos-like gains suggest a depth-scaling axis for looped LLMs and discusses stability, inference efficiency, and iso-FLOPs constraints for scaling loop architectures.

blogs.title: Exact Input Writes Improve Stable Looped Language Models
blogs.desc: Proposes replacing Parcae's Euler input-write gain with the exact zero-order-hold gain, then reports matched 140M looped-language-model controls where Exact-ZOH lowers validation loss under both short-budget probes and an 11.2B-token paper-style run.

blogs.title: Rethinking Hierarchy in Iterative Reasoning Models
blogs.desc: Interprets HRM/TRM-style two-level iteration through value-policy-like coupled dynamics, then reports preliminary evidence that low-level latent states can be better ACT halting inputs than answer-proximal high-level states.

briefings.title: Daily Loop-Model Watch — 2026-07-02
briefings.content:
# Daily Loop-Model Briefing - 2026-07-02

## Outcome

One new paper was added today: **G-RRM: Guiding Symbolic Solvers with Recurrent Reasoning Models** (`2607.02491`). It is not a general-purpose language-model architecture paper. Its value for this collection is narrower and cleaner: it tests whether a recurrent neural reasoner can provide useful guidance to exact symbolic solvers on constraint satisfaction problems.

## Added

### G-RRM: Guiding Symbolic Solvers with Recurrent Reasoning Models (`2607.02491`)

G-RRM uses symbol-equivariant Recurrent Reasoning Models (SE-RRMs) as neural solvers that generate full solution proposals, then passes those proposals as guidance to backtracking and SAT-style symbolic solvers. The paper reports that this works best when the symbolic solver has enough combinatorial search to benefit from hints and can dynamically overwrite bad branching choices.

Why it was added: SE-RRMs belong to the recurrent reasoning family this repository tracks: the learned solver repeatedly updates latent reasoning state rather than relying on an external prompt loop or plain autoregressive decoding. This paper is an applications-focused addition because the main result is about practical solver acceleration from that recurrent model, not a new base loop architecture.

Main reader takeaway: on 9x9 Sudoku, where the SE-RRM solves 91.1% of instances directly, G-RRM reduces median conflict counts to zero and reports a 33.3x median speedup for backtracking plus a 1.70x median speedup for Glucose 4.1. CaDiCaL does not benefit in the same way, which makes the paper useful as a boundary result: recurrent neural hints only translate into wall-clock gains when the downstream symbolic search procedure can exploit and repair them.

## Checked but not added

- **ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay as LLM Harness for Long-Context Reasoning** (`2607.02509`) is a training-free evidence replay method around an existing LLM. It uses model-internal relevance signals, but the loop is a context-selection and replay harness rather than a reused learned internal operator inside one forward process.
- **A Hippocampus for Linear Attention: An Exact Memory for What the Recurrent State Forgets** (`2607.02303`) improves sequence-memory behavior for linear attention with a bounded exact KV cache. It is adjacent recurrent-state work, but the abstract is about token-prefix memory rather than recurrent depth or fixed-point loop-model computation.
- **Multimodal Knowledge Edit-Scoped Generalization for Online Recursive MLLM Editing** (`2607.01978`) is an online editing method with recursive updates and preconditioners. The recurrence is in the editing/update procedure, not the model's same-forward learned-module computation.
- **Closed-loop, agentic, and human-in-the-loop papers** from today's feed were left out unless they exposed an internal reused learned operator. Their loops are control, planning, verification, data, or deployment workflows rather than loop-model mechanisms.

briefings.content:
# Daily Loop-Model Watch - 2026-07-01

One high-confidence paper was added today. The useful signal is not just that the paper studies latent reasoning; it directly builds on looped Transformers and proposes a new recurrence channel design for keeping multi-hop intermediate structure available across loop steps.

## DiscoLoop: Looping Discrete Embeddings and Continuous Hidden States for Multi-hop Reasoning

DiscoLoop studies two-hop reasoning where a model must compose parametric knowledge within a single forward pass instead of externalizing every step as chain-of-thought. The paper argues that standard non-recurrent Transformers lose useful first-hop facts across depth, while looped Transformers help by reusing memory but still suffer from representational mismatch between a decodable bridge entity and its token embedding.

Why it was added: the abstract explicitly frames Looped Transformers as the baseline mechanism and proposes a looping architecture whose recurrence carries both a discrete embedding channel and a continuous hidden-state channel. That makes the repeated learned computation part of the model's forward process, not an external prompting, search, or agent loop.

Reader takeaway: the mixed-channel recurrence nearly solves the symbolic and synthetic-language multi-hop setting with fewer training steps, and the paper reports lower training loss plus stronger benchmark performance than looped-transformer baselines in real-world pretraining.

## Checked But Not Added

- **Agentic generation of verifiable rules for deterministic, self-expanding reaction classification** (`2607.01061`) uses a verification loop in a multi-agent chemistry workflow. The loop is external rule generation and testing, not a reused learned internal operator.
- **Monodromy in Persistent Homology over 1-Manifolds in 2D** (`2607.01046`) is mathematically about closed loops in topological data analysis. It has no loop-model architecture connection.

briefings.summary: Two newly announced loop-model papers were added: LOTUS uses looped Transformers for supervised latent reasoning, and CHERRY studies recurrent recovery after deep Transformer compression. Several latent-reasoning and feature-refinement papers were checked but left out because they do not clearly reuse a learned internal operator.
briefings.content:
# Daily Loop-Model Watch - 2026-06-30

Two high-confidence papers were added today. Both are language-model papers, but they hit different parts of the loop-model boundary: one uses recurrent-depth Transformers to make latent reasoning competitive with explicit CoT, while the other uses recurrent unrolling as a way to recover capability after aggressive depth compression.

## Bridging the Gap Between Latent and Explicit Reasoning with Looped Transformers

LOTUS starts from a practical weakness of latent chain-of-thought methods: they can be faster than decoded reasoning, but have tended to fall behind explicit CoT as models scale. The paper's answer is a looped padded Transformer that processes `K` latent blocks in parallel for `R` recurrent iterations, with per-latent-position supervision against gold CoT-step tokens.

Why it was added: the abstract explicitly identifies looped, recurrent-depth Transformers as the mechanism, and says those Transformers reuse weights to increase computation depth without adding parameters. That is the collection's core same-forward learned-operator reuse rule.

Reader takeaway: latent reasoning does not have to be a black-box speed hack. With looped recurrence plus direct token-level supervision on latent positions, the paper reports matching explicit CoT at 3B scale while cutting thought-phase latency by 2.5x-6.9x.

## CHERRY: Compressed Hierarchical Experts with Recurrent Representational Yield

CHERRY is framed as a set of compute-efficiency techniques for language models. The loop-model part is the depth-compression result: a 48-layer, 1B-parameter Transformer is compressed to 6 layers, then restored through learned recurrent unrolling to 34 effective recurrent layers. The paper also combines compressed models as efficient experts.

Why it was added: recurrent unrolling is central to the reported depth recovery, and the abstract presents it as a learned mechanism for regaining effective depth after compression rather than as a training loop or repeated full-model call.

Reader takeaway: this is a useful efficiency-side data point for looped language models. The reported loss is close to a larger dense model while using fewer parameters, though the evidence is explicitly limited to one Korean model family and loss-based evaluation.

## Checked But Not Added

- **CoLT: Teaching Multi-Modal Models to Think with Chain of Latent Thoughts** (`2606.31986`) is a strong latent-reasoning paper, but the abstract describes chains of latent thought representations and supervisory decoders rather than a looped/reused internal learned block.
- **FR-DETR** (`2606.30471`) includes a Recurrent Focus Refinement Module for object detection under adverse weather. It is adjacent, but the abstract only says features are iteratively refined using coarse predictions as guidance; it does not make shared learned-operator reuse explicit enough for this collection.
- **Flow Reasoning Models** (`2606.29150`) uses discrete flow denoising, self-conditioning, and dynamically stable candidates as verifiers for structured reasoning. It is close in fixed-point language, but the loop is flow/denoising inference plus candidate search, not a learned recurrent-depth block reused as the model architecture.
- **CARVE** (`2606.27229`) remains a near-miss from the prior watch: it improves recurrent linear attention over sequence-time memory, not same-forward depth reuse.

Several same-day reasoning/refinement papers were checked but left out because their loops are external, diffusion/search-based, or ordinary sequence recurrence.
briefings.content:
# Daily Loop-Model Watch - 2026-06-29

One high-confidence paper was added today. The useful signal is narrow but strong: it directly studies looped Transformers, where the same Transformer block is applied repeatedly, and asks how to make extrapolation across loop counts less brittle.

## Stabilizing Extrapolation in Looped Transformers via Learned Stochastic Stopping

This paper targets a specific failure mode in looped Transformers. These models can solve variable-length algorithmic tasks by increasing the number of loop applications at inference time, but the paper reports that solutions with good in-distribution accuracy can still show high out-of-distribution variance when sequence length and loop count shift together.

The proposed fix is to treat loop count as a training-time stochastic variable. Randomizing the number of loops during training reduces the shortcut between sequence length and loop count, and the authors also study RL-Halting as a learned stochastic stopping schedule. Across binary addition, Dyck-1, Unique Set, and Copy, learned stochastic stopping often improves the accuracy-stability tradeoff, though it can also stabilize a suboptimal computation.

Why it was added: the architecture is squarely inside the collection boundary. The abstract explicitly says looped Transformers repeatedly apply a shared Transformer block, and the paper analyzes how stochastic loop counts and learned stopping affect that reused-block computation. This is not repeated prompting, agent planning, or a full-model retry loop.

Reader takeaway: "when to stop" is not just a test-time allocation policy for looped Transformers; it changes what computation the recurrent block learns during training.

## Checked But Not Added

- **Latent Visual Diffusion Reasoning with Monte Carlo Tree Search** (`2606.27988`) combines visual diffusion reasoning with MCTS. It is an inference/search process around diffusion-style reasoning, not same-forward reuse of a learned internal block.
- **Grounded Iterative Language Planning** (`2606.27806`) combines a parameterized world model with API-based agent reasoning. The loop is an agent planning workflow, outside the repo's internal-recurrence boundary.
- **Towards Reliable and Robust LLM Planning** (`2606.27757`) uses symbolic feedback for iterative self-refinement. That is prompt/framework iteration over LLM calls, not a looped model architecture.
- **MER-R1** (`2606.27652`) optimizes slow-fast multimodal emotion reasoning behavior. The abstract does not identify a reused internal learned operator within one forward process.
- **Speculative Refinement** (`2606.27474`) is a hybrid autoregressive/diffusion decoding strategy. It is a decoding/refinement method, not a recurrent-depth or fixed-point model.
- **CARVE** (`2606.27229`) remains adjacent but outside the strict boundary: it improves recurrent linear attention over sequence-time memory, not looped depth inside one forward reasoning process.

briefings.summary: No new strict-scope papers were added; the closest recent papers used predictive-coding inference, latent-reasoning initialization, sequence-time recurrence, or external agent loops rather than reused internal learned modules.
highlights: The strongest technical near-miss was a predictive-coding paper that compares iterative sparse inference schemes, but its loop is an optimization/inference procedure over a fixed energy.
Recent latent-reasoning and recurrent-transformer papers remain useful vocabulary signals, yet their abstracts do not establish same-forward reuse of a learned block as the central mechanism.
Agent loops, diffusion loops, rollout loops, and sequence-memory recurrence continue to be excluded unless they expose reused internal learned operators.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing - 2026-06-28

## Outcome

No paper crossed the strict collection boundary today. Recent arXiv listings contained several papers with loop, recurrent, predictive-coding, and latent-reasoning language, but none clearly made a learned layer, block, module, or fixed-point operator get reused within one model forward process.

The prior strict-scope recursive-reasoner compression paper (`2606.26488`) is already covered by the existing June 25 review branch, so it was not duplicated here.

## Checked but not added

- **Accelerating Hierarchical Sparse Predictive Coding with Hybrid Amortized Inference** (`2606.27802`) is the closest technical near-miss. It studies hierarchical sparse predictive coding and compares ISTA, MFISTA, a LISTA-style amortized encoder, and hybrid corrective refinement. That is relevant to iterative latent inference, but the loop in the abstract is an optimization/inference procedure over a fixed sparse energy rather than a learned block or module reused as model depth.
- **Intuition-Guided Latent Reasoning for LLM-Based Recommendation** (`2606.27684`) proposes an intuition embedding that initializes latent preference reasoning for recommendation. The paper is adjacent to continuous latent reasoning, but the abstract emphasizes starting-point alignment and recommendation-specific guidance, not recurrent reuse of an internal learned operator.
- **The Context-Ready Transformer** (`2606.27538`) introduces a recurrent architecture around a Transformer block and correction network. The recurrence is tied to left-to-right sequence context and cached previous-position outputs; the abstract does not present recurrent depth or same-forward reuse of a shared block as the collection's target mechanism.
- **Semantic Early-Stopping for Iterative LLM Agent Loops** (`2606.27009`) is a clean example of an excluded agent/workflow loop. It stops Writer/Critic-style multi-agent drafting loops based on semantic convergence, but repeated external LLM calls are outside the repo scope.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing - 2026-06-25

## Outcome

One new paper was added to the collection. It is not a new looped architecture proposal, but it directly studies what happens when recursive reasoning models are compressed for edge deployment. The key loop-model issue is trajectory preservation: recursive models repeatedly update a latent state, so small compression errors can compound across reasoning cycles and break global task success even when local predictions still look acceptable.

## Added

### What Survives When You Compress a Recursive Reasoner for the Edge? (`2606.26488`)

This paper asks how recursive reasoning models behave under deployment-oriented compression. The authors test precision sweeps, pruning, distillation, linear attention, calibrated INT4, and edge memory constraints across structured tasks and recursive architectures.

Why it was added: the subject is explicitly recursive reasoning models that solve structured tasks by repeatedly updating a latent state. The paper studies the failure mode that matters for loop models: whether the recurrent reasoning trajectory survives compression. It belongs in analysis because the main contribution is diagnostic and deployment-oriented rather than a new base architecture.

Main reader takeaway: naive compression can preserve cell-level/local accuracy while collapsing puzzle-exact/global reasoning to zero. Carry-trajectory fidelity is introduced as a label-free signal for whether the compressed model still follows the full-precision reasoning path, and calibrated INT4 is reported to recover the failure without retraining.

## Checked but not added

- **CARVE: Content-Aware Recurrent with Value Efficiency for Chunk-Parallel Linear Attention** (`2606.27229`) improves recurrent sequence memory for delta-rule linear attention. It is useful adjacent work, but the recurrence is over token/state memory in a sequence model rather than reused depth within one forward reasoning process.
- **Erase-then-Delta Attention: Decoupling Erase and Write Addresses in Delta-Rule Linear Attention** (`2606.26560`) adds a learned erase direction to recurrent memory updates. It is again sequence-memory architecture, not a looped internal block or fixed-point/recurrent-depth model.
- **Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers** (`2606.27354`) iteratively corrects PDE predictions by feeding residual fields back into a learned solver. The learned update policy is interesting, but the abstract frames it as solver iteration for PDE surrogates rather than the loop-model families this repo tracks.
- **E-TTS: A New Embodied Test-Time Scaling Framework for Robotic Manipulation** (`2606.27268`) uses history-aware iterative refinement with vision-language verifiers. The loop is a modular test-time framework around candidate sampling and verification, not reuse of a learned internal module inside one model forward pass.
- **World Action Models Enable Continual Imitation Learning with Recurrent Generative Replays** (`2606.27374`) recursively queries a world-action model to synthesize replay trajectories. That is repeated external model querying for continual learning, outside the strict same-forward reused-module boundary.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing - 2026-06-24

## Outcome

No paper crossed the strict collection boundary today. The strongest signals were about attractor dynamics, associative-memory steering, long-horizon latent prediction, diffusion cycling, and external verifier loops. Those are adjacent to the language of iterative computation, but none made a learned layer, block, module, or fixed-point operator get reused within one model forward process.

## Checked but not added

- **Reasoning as Attractor Dynamics: Latent Memory Retrieval via Gibbs-Weighted Energy Minimization** (`2606.24543`) models correct reasoning chains as attractor basins and weights sampled reasoning paths by inverse energy. It is adjacent to loop-model inference, but the repeated object is sampled output trajectories, not an internal learned module.
- **Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping** (`2606.24396`) learns a state-dependent steering field for frozen Transformer activations. The paper uses attractor and equilibrium language, but the mechanism is adaptation/steering of a single pass rather than recurrence over a reused block.
- **Learning the Koopman Operator using Attention Free Transformers** (`2606.23957`) combines a Koopman autoencoder with an attention-free latent memory block and optional dynamic re-encoding. It is a relevant memory-and-rollout paper, but the loop is temporal prediction plus drift correction, not recurrent-depth reuse in one forward computation.
- **Cyclic Denoising Reveals Ultrastable Memories in Diffusion Models** (`2606.24000`) repeatedly applies forward and reverse diffusion to expose memorized attractors. Diffusion cycling and sampler control are explicitly outside this collection's strict scope.
- **Closing the Loop: Formally Verified Law as a Reward Signal for Self-Improving Legal AI** (`2606.23913`) closes a training/reward loop with an external verifier. That is an agent/training workflow loop, not a loop-model architecture.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing - 2026-06-23

## Outcome

One paper was added to the collection. It is not a language-model looped Transformer, but it fits the same strict computational pattern: a learned recurrent update is applied repeatedly inside one model process, and the number of recurrence steps becomes a test-time compute knob.

## Added to the collection

### Adaptive Recurrent Message Passing for Test Time Computing on Graphs

AdaR targets graph learning, where fixed-depth architectures can be too rigid across different graph tasks. The paper derives step dependence as the condition needed for adaptive convergence, then builds a recurrent graph model whose updates encode normalized step information and representation-target relations.

Why it was added: the central mechanism is recurrent learned message passing. AdaR runs repeated internal representation updates without changing model parameters, making recurrence depth a flexible test-time compute axis rather than an external agent loop or repeated full-model call.

Main reader takeaway: this is a graph-domain example of loop-model compute scaling. The interesting part for this collection is the explicit recurrent update design and convergence supervision, not just better graph benchmark numbers.

## Checked but not added

- **Reasoning as Attractor Dynamics: Latent Memory Retrieval via Gibbs-Weighted Energy Minimization** (`2606.24543`) frames LLM reasoning as settling into attractor basins, but the operational mechanism samples multiple output reasoning paths and reweights them by an energy score. That is adjacent inference-time aggregation, not a reused learned internal module.
- **Compressing Observation History into Agent Memory: Distilling Transformers into Recurrent Transformers** (`2606.21562`) is useful recurrent-memory work for streaming vision and robotics, but the abstract emphasizes sequence-memory compression over time rather than recurrent-depth reuse within one forward process.
- **Compression and Retrieval: Implicit Memory Retrieval for Video World Models** (`2606.23105`) adds attention-driven implicit memory retrieval plus context compression for video world models. Retrieval over compressed context is not enough by itself to meet the strict loop-model criterion.
- **IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation** (`2606.24849`) performs latent visual planning in a single forward pass, but the abstract does not identify a reused internal learned block or fixed-point computation.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing - 2026-06-22

## Outcome

No new paper was added to the main collection today. The latest arXiv listings still stop at the Friday, June 19 announcement, and the strongest fresh signals were adjacent rather than in scope: theory about Chain-of-Thought computation, a broad architecture that can express recurrence, and feedback-aware conditional flows.

## Strongest watchlist signals

### Efficiently Representing Algorithms With Chain-of-Thought Transformers

This paper asks whether Chain-of-Thought Transformers can simulate Word RAM algorithms efficiently, rather than only Turing machines. It also studies continuous CoT and a hybrid setting with Transformer layers above a recurrent linear-RNN layer.

Why it was not added: the central compute loop is the generated reasoning trace or a theory construction around CoT simulation. The abstract does not present a looped, weight-tied, fixed-point, or reused internal learned block as the model mechanism to collect.

### ITNet: A Learnable Integral Transform That Subsumes Convolution, Attention, and Recurrence

ITNet proposes a learnable integral-transform architecture whose kernel can express convolution, self-attention, and autoregressive recurrence as special cases. The result is a broad unifying operator view with shared components across modalities.

Why it was not added: recurrence is discussed as an expressible special case, not as a same-forward recurrent-depth or implicit-layer mechanism. The paper is interesting architecture theory, but it does not cross the repo's conservative reused-module intake boundary.

### FlowBender: Feedback-Aware Training for Self-Correcting Conditional Flows

FlowBender trains conditional diffusion and flow models to consume their own alignment error during refinement. It uses look-ahead estimates and task-specific deviations to correct generation under image-to-image, restoration, and texturing constraints.

Why it was not added: the feedback loop is a conditional flow or diffusion-style refinement procedure. The repository excludes diffusion or energy-style iterative inference unless the paper's central contribution is a reused learned internal loop-model operator.

## Already covered

### Recurrent neural networks approximate continuous functions

This remains the clearest strict-scope signal in the Friday batch: a single fixed-weight recurrent network can improve approximation accuracy by running longer. It is already in the collection, so no duplicate entry was added today.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing — 2026-06-21

## Outcome

No new paper was added to the main collection today. The newest arXiv batch surfaced useful adjacent work on latent reasoning, diffusion-model transparency, and coding-agent guidance, but none crossed the repository's strict boundary: within one model forward process, a learned layer, block, module, or operator should be reused as the central computation.

## Strongest watchlist signal

### What Makes Effective Supervision in Latent Chain-of-Thought

This paper analyzes why latent Chain-of-Thought training can drift when only outcome supervision is available. It separates supervision into trajectory signals and latent-space structure, then introduces a probe for measuring how much information latent trajectories retain about explicit reasoning steps.

Why it was not added: the paper is about supervising and measuring latent reasoning traces. The abstract does not identify a looped, weight-tied, recurrent-depth, or fixed-point architecture that reuses a learned internal operator within a single forward process. It is good context for latent reasoning evaluation, not a conservative loop-model intake.

## Other near-misses

### How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?

DiffusionGemma is relevant because it studies reasoning transparency when computation happens across denoising steps in continuous latent space. The paper asks whether intermediate denoising states can be made interpretable and reports that token-bottleneck mappings can reduce opaque serial depth without hurting downstream performance.

Why it was not added: the loop is diffusion denoising, where the model changes token predictions over sampling steps. The repo explicitly excludes diffusion or energy-style iterative inference unless the paper's central contribution is a reused learned internal loop-model operator.

### Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents

This paper tunes repository guidance files by running synthetic bug-fix probes and patching the guidance from the observed failures. It is practical context for agent workflows and instruction quality.

Why it was not added: the refinement happens outside the model as a coding-agent or prompt-guidance workflow. It is not a model architecture with same-forward reuse of a learned layer, block, module, or operator.

# Daily Loop-Model Briefing — 2026-06-18

## Outcome

One paper was added to the collection. It is a theory-forward recurrent-computation paper rather than a new Transformer architecture: the contribution is an approximation theorem showing that a fixed recurrent network can trade more runtime for more accuracy.

## Added to the collection

### Recurrent neural networks approximate continuous functions

This paper asks whether improving approximation accuracy always requires choosing a larger or newly parameterized neural network. Its answer is no: for every continuous function on `[-1,1]`, the authors prove that one fixed ReLU recurrent neural network with fixed hidden dimension can approximate the function uniformly, with better accuracy obtained by letting the same recurrent dynamics run longer.

Why it was added: the central object is a shared recurrent transition reused over time. The paper is not about repeated prompting, external agent loops, or autoregressive decoding; it treats the repeated application of one fixed-weight recurrent network as the computational resource. That makes it a theory-side flat-loop entry for the collection.

Main reader takeaway: the result frames runtime as an unavoidable approximation resource in a fixed-network setting. This complements looped-transformer and recurrent-depth work by giving a clean theoretical example where repeated shared computation, rather than additional parameters, buys accuracy.

## Checked but not added

- **Learning to Refine Hidden States for Reliable LLM Reasoning** (`2606.17524`) is adjacent latent-refinement work, but the abstract does not clearly identify a reused learned internal layer, block, module, or operator as the central mechanism.
- **What Makes Effective Supervision in Latent Chain-of-Thought: An Information-Theoretic Analysis** (`2606.20075`) analyzes latent reasoning supervision and mutual-information probes, but it is not a new looped or implicit-layer architecture intake.
- **Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents** (`2606.20512`) studies an external repository-guidance tuning loop for coding agents, so it is outside the same-forward internal loop-model scope.
